content: DRAWDOWN RECOVERY MATHEMATICS — The Asymmetric Trap: Losses compound against a diminishing capital base. Recovery gains required: -10% drawdown = +11.1% to recover (~1.1 years at 10% annual). -20% = +25% (~2.5 years). -30% = +42.9% (~3.8 years). -50% = +100% (~7.3 years). -75% = +300% (~14+ years). -90% = +900% (~24+ years). THIS IS WHY CAPITAL PRESERVATION IS MORE IMPORTANT THAN MAXIMIZING RETURNS. A trader who avoids a 50% drawdown gains 7 years of compounding vs one who doesn't. Every risk management decision should be viewed through this lens.

KELLY CRITERION — Optimal Position Sizing: f* = (bp - q) / b. Where b = average win / average loss (payoff odds), p = win probability, q = 1-p (loss probability). Example: 60% win rate, 1.34 R:R → f* = (1.34 × 0.6 - 0.4) / 1.34 = 0.30 (30% of bankroll). Kelly MAXIMIZES long-term geometric growth over infinite time. BUT: assumes static probabilities and zero slippage. Markets are non-stationary. Full Kelly = catastrophic short-term volatility. NEVER use full Kelly in live trading.

FRACTIONAL KELLY — Practical Position Sizing: Use HALF Kelly (f*/2) or QUARTER Kelly (f*/4). Half Kelly sacrifices ~25% of theoretical growth for DRAMATICALLY smoother equity curve. Quarter Kelly sacrifices ~44% of growth for near-zero ruin probability. Why fractional: 1) Win rate estimates have error bars — full Kelly amplifies estimation errors. 2) Markets are non-stationary — edge degrades over time. 3) Drawdown from full Kelly is psychologically devastating and causes tilt. Rule of thumb: Start at quarter Kelly. Increase to half Kelly only after 100+ trades with validated edge. Never exceed half Kelly.

ANTI-MARTINGALE — Scale with Equity, Not Against It: NEVER double down on losers (Martingale). Martingale produces smooth equity curves in ranging markets but ONE extended trend wipes out the entire account. Anti-Martingale: position size = f(account equity). As equity decreases (losing streak), position size naturally decreases. As equity increases (winning streak), position size naturally increases. This aligns exposure with market regimes where your strategy has maximum efficacy. Implementation: recalculate position size on EVERY trade based on CURRENT equity, not starting equity.

ATR TRAILING STOP — Chandelier Exit: Unlike rigid percentage stops that ignore market context, ATR stops adapt to prevailing volatility. Implementation: Long entry → trailing stop = Highest High(22) - 3×ATR(22). Short entry → trailing stop = Lowest Low(22) + 3×ATR(22). As volatility expands, stop widens to prevent premature wicks. As trend accelerates and volatility compresses, stop tightens to lock in profits. The 3× multiplier can be adjusted: 2× = tighter (more whipsaws), 4× = looser (larger drawdowns). This removes ALL discretionary emotion from the exit sequence.

RISK OF RUIN — Probability of Account Destruction: Risk of Ruin = ((1-edge) / (1+edge))^units. Where edge = (win_rate × avg_win) - (loss_rate × avg_loss). Even a positive-edge strategy has non-zero ruin probability if position sizing is too aggressive. Example: 55% win rate, 1:1 R:R, risking 10% per trade → ruin probability ~35%. Same system risking 1% per trade → ruin probability <0.1%. THE SINGLE BIGGEST FACTOR IN RISK OF RUIN IS POSITION SIZE, NOT WIN RATE. A 70% win rate system risking 20% per trade has HIGHER ruin risk than a 50% win rate system risking 1% per trade.

PORTFOLIO HEAT — Total Open Risk Management: Heat = total unrealized risk across all open positions as % of equity. Rule: NEVER exceed 6% total portfolio heat at any time. If 3 positions open, each risking 1% = 3% heat (safe). If one position moves to break-even, heat drops to 2%. If correlation between positions is high (e.g., BTC and ETH), reduce max heat to 4%. Monitor heat in real-time. If heat exceeds threshold, CLOSE the lowest-conviction position first. Heat management prevents correlated drawdowns from wiping out the account.

CORRELATION-BASED POSITION SIZING: When holding correlated assets (BTC + ETH correlation >0.8), the ACTUAL risk is 2× the perceived risk. If you risk 1% on BTC and 1% on ETH, and they're 90% correlated, your effective risk is ~1.9%, not 2%. Rule: If correlation >0.7, reduce position size on the second asset by 50%. If correlation >0.9, only hold ONE of the pair. During high volatility, correlations tend to spike to 1.0 — exactly when you think diversification is protecting you, it isn't. Monitor 30-day rolling correlation.

VOLATILITY-ADJUSTED POSITION SIZING: Position size should INVERSELY correlate with volatility. High volatility (ATR > 1.5× average) → reduce position size by 50%. Low volatility (ATR < 0.7× average) → can increase size by 20%. Formula: position_size = base_size × (avg_atr / current_atr). This ensures consistent dollar risk regardless of volatility regime. In high vol, smaller positions with wider stops. In low vol, larger positions with tighter stops. The RESULT is the same dollar risk per trade, but the EXPERIENCE is fewer whipsaws.

MAXIMUM FAVORABLE EXCURSION (MFE) ANALYSIS: MFE = the maximum profit a trade reaches before being closed. Track MFE for all trades to optimize exits. If average MFE is 3R but you're only capturing 1.5R = you're leaving 50% of edge on the table. If MFE distribution shows most trades reach 2R before reversing = set TP at 2R. If MFE shows wide distribution = use trailing stops instead of fixed TPs. MFE analysis turns subjective exit decisions into data-driven rules. Review MFE monthly to adjust exit strategy.

RISK-REWARD RATIO OPTIMIZATION: Minimum R:R should be calculated from win rate, not assumed. Break-even R:R = (1 - win_rate) / win_rate. Example: 40% win rate → need 1.5:1 R:R minimum. 50% win rate → need 1:1 minimum. 60% win rate → need 0.67:1 minimum. NEVER take a trade where the R:R is below your break-even threshold. Higher R:R allows lower win rate and vice versa. The optimal combination depends on your edge: if you have high accuracy, tighter R:R is fine. If low accuracy, need wider R:R. Don't force 1:3 R:R if your edge is 70% win rate with 1:1.

DRAWDOWN CIRCUIT BREAKER THRESHOLDS: Implement 3-tier circuit breaker system. Tier 1: 3% daily loss → reduce position size by 50% for next 3 trades. Tier 2: 5% weekly loss → stop trading for 48 hours, review all trades. Tier 3: 10% from peak equity → full stop, manual restart required, review entire strategy. Circuit breakers are INDEPENDENT of the AI brain — the risk layer cannot be overridden by the agent. Reset circuit breakers only after review is complete. The purpose is to prevent TILT from compounding into catastrophic drawdowns.

SCALE-OUT MATHEMATICS — Partial Exit Optimization: Scale-out reduces variance at the cost of expected value. Optimal scale-out depends on win rate and R:R. High win rate (60%+) → scale out less (keep more runner). Low win rate (<50%) → scale out more (secure profits). Common framework: TP1 at 1:1 → close 50%, move SL to break-even. TP2 at 1:2 → close 30%. TP3 at 1:3 → close 20%. This captures ~70% of expected value while dramatically reducing variance. The runner (20%) captures tail events. Adjust percentages based on your MFE analysis.

BREAK-EVEN STOP: When It Helps vs Hurts: Moving stop to break-even after TP1 hit is STANDARD PRACTICE but has nuance. Helps: In trending markets, protects against reversal after partial profit. Prevents winning trades from becoming losers. Hurts: In volatile markets, break-even stops get wicked out before price continues in your direction. If your win rate is high (>60%), break-even stops reduce EV because you're converting winners into scratch trades. Rule: Use break-even in trending regimes (ADX > 25). Skip break-even in ranging regimes. Always move to break-even AFTER first scale-out, not before.
